session.user:
Fix test files that reference renamed symbols. These are test failures from a plan_number/plan_id to pr_number/pr_id rename sweep.
DO NOT modify any src/ files. ONLY modify test files.
Category 1: exit_plan_mode_hook test - File: tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_exit_plan_mode_hook.py - Issue: Imports `extract_plan_title` which was renamed to `extract_pr_title` in the source - Also may have references to old dataclass field names: `plan_saved_plan_number` → `plan_saved_pr_number`, `plan_title` → `pr_title`, `plan_number` → `pr_number_from_plan_ref` - Fix: Update all imports and references
Category 2: PrViewResult in view/operation.py tests - File: tests/commands/pr/test_view.py and tests/commands/test_top_level_commands.py - Issue: Code constructs PrViewResult with `plan_id` field, but it was renamed to `pr_id` in src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/operation.py - First READ the PrViewResult dataclass in src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/operation.py to see the current field name - Fix: Update test files to use the new field name
Category 3: land_execute() signature - Files: tests/commands/land/test_git_pull.py, test_learn_skip_remote.py, test_objective_update.py, test_unresolved_comments.py - Issue: `land_execute()` in src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/land_execute.py had `plan_number` renamed to `linked_pr_number` - First READ the land_execute.py file to see the current parameter name - Fix: Update all test calls to use the new parameter name
Category 4: LearnResult initialization - File: tests/commands/learn/test_display.py - Issue: Creates LearnResult with `plan_id=` but field was renamed to `pr_id=` - Fix: Update test to use `pr_id=`
IMPORTANT: For each file, READ it first, then make the minimum necessary edits. Don't change anything not related to these renames.
session.assistant: I'll fix the test files that reference renamed symbols. Let me start by reading the affected test files and the source files they depend on to understand the current state.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Core operation for pr view (transport-independent). 2→ 3→Contains the request type and operation function that both the 4→human command (pr/view/cli.py) and machine command (pr/view/json_cli.py) share. 5→""" 6→ 7→from dataclasses import dataclass 8→from datetime import datetime 9→from typing import Any 10→ 11→from erk.cli.github_parsing import parse_issue_identifier 12→from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github 13→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 14→from erk_shared.agentclick.machine_command import MachineCommandError 15→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import BRANCH_NAME 18→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 19→from erk_shared.plan_store.conversion import github_issue_to_plan 20→from erk_shared.plan_store.types import PlanNotFound 21→ 22→ 23→@dataclass(frozen=True) 24→class PrViewRequest: 25→ """Request type for pr view.""" 26→ 27→ identifier: str | None = None 28→ full: bool = False 29→ 30→

1→"""Execute deferred land operations from activation script. 2→ 3→This exec command performs the actual merge and cleanup operations for landing a PR. 4→It is called by the activation script after the user has approved the operation. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk exec land-execute --pr-number=123 --branch=foo [options] 8→ 9→All validation happens during the validation phase (erk land). This command 10→executes the pre-validated operations. 11→ 12→Exit Codes: 13→ 0: Success (PR merged, branch deleted) 14→ 1: Error (merge failed, validation error) 15→""" 16→ 17→from pathlib import Path 18→ 19→import click 20→ 21→from erk.cli.commands.land_cmd import _execute_land 22→from erk.cli.commands.objective_helpers import run_objective_update_after_land 23→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_context 24→ 25→ 26→@click.command(name="land-execute") 27→@click.option( 28→ "--pr-number", 29→ type=int, 30→ required=True,

1→"""Tests for skipping learn prompt on remote PRs. 2→ 3→When landing a PR via `erk land <url>` or `erk land <number>` for a branch 4→that has NO local worktree, the learn status check should be skipped because 5→there are no local Claude sessions to learn from. 6→ 7→Regression test for issue #4871. 8→""" 9→ 10→import pytest 11→from click.testing import CliRunner 12→ 13→from erk.cli.cli import cli 14→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo 16→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata 17→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 18→from tests.fakes.gateway.github import FakeLocalGitHub 19→from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues 20→from tests.fakes.gateway.graphite import FakeGraphite 21→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env 22→ 23→ 24→def test_land_skips_learn_prompt_for_remote_pr( 25→ monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, 26→) -> None: 27→ """Test that learn prompt is skipped when landing a PR with no local worktree. 28→ 29→ When running `erk land <url>` for a remote PR (no local worktree), the learn 30→ status check should not run because there are no local Claude sessions to

1→"""Tests for erk land command unresolved review comments warnings. 2→ 3→Tests for behavior when PRs have unresolved review comments: 4→- Warning shown with prompt to continue 5→- Execute mode skips the warning (no prompts in execute phase) 6→- User can confirm to proceed despite warning 7→- Non-interactive mode fails with error when unresolved comments exist 8→""" 9→ 10→from click.testing import CliRunner 11→ 12→from erk.cli.cli import cli 13→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PRReviewThread, PullRequestInfo 15→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata 16→from tests.fakes.gateway.console import FakeConsole 17→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 18→from tests.fakes.gateway.github import FakeLocalGitHub 19→from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues 20→from tests.fakes.gateway.graphite import FakeGraphite 21→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env 22→ 23→ 24→def test_land_warns_on_unresolved_comments() -> None: 25→ """Test land shows warning when PR has unresolved review comments.""" 26→ runner = CliRunner() 27→ with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: 28→ repo_dir = env.setup_repo_structure() 29→ 30→ git_ops = FakeGit(

1→"""Tests for erk land command git pull behavior. 2→ 3→By default, landing navigates to trunk and executes git pull in Python. 4→The --no-pull flag and --up flag affect this behavior. 5→ 6→The git pull is executed in Python (not in the activation script) to avoid 7→race conditions with stale index.lock files from earlier git operations. 8→""" 9→ 10→from pathlib import Path 11→ 12→from click.testing import CliRunner 13→ 14→from erk.cli.cli import cli 15→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 16→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import BranchDivergence 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo 18→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata 19→from tests.fakes.gateway.git import FakeGit 20→from tests.fakes.gateway.github import FakeLocalGitHub 21→from tests.fakes.gateway.github_issues import FakeGitHubIssues 22→from tests.fakes.gateway.graphite import FakeGraphite 23→from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env 24→ 25→ 26→def test_land_default_calls_git_pull_in_python() -> None: 27→ """Test default land (no --no-pull) calls git pull during execute phase.""" 28→ runner = CliRunner() 29→ with erk_inmem_env(runner) as env: 30→ repo_dir = env.setup_repo_structure()
... [882 lines omitted] ...
124→ catch_exceptions=False, 217→ catch_exceptions=False, 316→ cli, ["land", "--script", "--up", "--force"], obj=test_ctx, catch_exceptions=False 335→def test_land_git_pull_failure_shows_warning_but_succeeds() -> None: 336→ """Test that git pull failure shows a warning but doesn't fail the command.""" 356→ # Simulate git pull failure with RuntimeError (wrapped by run_subprocess_with_context) 357→ pull_branch_raises=RuntimeError("git pull failed"), 421→ # Execute mode: test git pull failure behavior during execution phase 435→ catch_exceptions=False, 438→ # Command should still succeed (exit 0) despite git pull failure 444→ # Verify warning message was shown 445→ assert "Warning:" in result.output 446→ assert "git pull failed" in result.output 450→ """Test that land detects diverged branch (ahead AND behind) and skips pull with warning.""" 547→ catch_exceptions=False, 559→ # Verify divergence warning was shown 560→ assert "Warning:" in result.output 665→ catch_exceptions=False, 779→ catch_exceptions=False, 795→def test_land_pull_failure_after_divergence_check_shows_warning() -> None: 796→ """Test that pull failure shows warning even after divergence check passes.""" 817→ pull_branch_raises=RuntimeError("Unexpected pull failure"), 881→ # Execute mode: test pull failure handling 895→ catch_exceptions=False, 898→ # Command should succeed (pull failure is non-fatal) 904→ # Verify warning message was shown 905→ assert "Warning:" in result.output 906→ assert "git pull failed" in result.output

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>
session.assistant: I see! There are TWO `--pr-number` options - one on line 28 and another on line 58. The second one maps to `linked_pr_number`. The tests don't need to be changed - they're using the right CLI option. The issue is that the code internally passes `linked_pr_number` to `_execute_land` but uses a different parameter name there. Let me check what parameter name `_execute_land` expects:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Check for plan_number usage in land_execute.py

session.tool_result: 185: plan_number=linked_pr_number,
session.assistant:
Excellent! So the source code is correct - it passes `linked_pr_number` as `plan_number` to `_execute_land`. The tests are also correct as they use the CLI option `--pr-number` which maps to the second occurrence. The tests should work as-is for land_execute tests.
Now let me run the tests to verify all my changes work:

tool_use.param: Run exit_plan_mode_hook tests to verify fixes

session.assistant:
Ah! The issue is that there are TWO `--pr-number` options in land_execute.py (lines 27-31 and 57-61), which is causing the warning and the parameter isn't being passed. The test needs to pass `--pr-number` for BOTH parameters. Looking at the source code more carefully:
- Line 27-31: `--pr-number` for `pr_number` (PR to merge) - Line 57-61: `--pr-number` for `linked_pr_number` (Linked PR for objective update)
This is a duplicate parameter name which Click doesn't support well. But wait, I was told NOT to modify src/ files. Let me check if the tests need to pass a different value. Let me look at the test invocations more carefully:
Looking at the test, it's calling: ``` "--pr-number=123", ```
But there are two `--pr-number` options in the source. The issue is that both can't be named the same. Let me check if this was already an issue or if something else changed. Let me look at what the tests are actually trying to do - they're testing that objective_number parameter is passed, not the linked_pr_number. Actually, the tests don't need to pass the linked PR number at all for these tests since they're not testing objective update functionality.
Let me check the tests - do they actually need `--pr-number` twice?
Looking at test_objective_update.py, it passes `--objective-number=100` but not a separate linked PR. The tests in the other files don't pass objective numbers. So the duplicate `--pr-number` in the source is a bug, but I was told not to modify src/ files.
Let me re-read the instructions - I need to check if the issue is actually in the source or the tests. Looking at line 58 of land_execute.py from my earlier read:
```python @click.option( "--pr-number", type=int, help="Linked PR number", ) ```
This should probably be `--linked-pr-number` instead of `--pr-number`. But I'm not allowed to modify src/. However, this IS the cause of the test failure. Let me check if perhaps this was recently changed and the tests just need updating. Let me check git history or comments about this:
Actually, looking at the user's instructions again: "These are test failures from a plan_number/plan_id to pr_number/pr_id rename sweep." So this IS part of the rename sweep - someone renamed plan_number to linked_pr_number but forgot to also rename the Click option from `--plan-number` to `--linked-pr-number`.
Since I can't modify src/, I need to report this issue. But wait - let me check if the option name was actually changed in the source. Let me look at the source more carefully:
From my earlier read at line 58, it says `--pr-number` for the second option. But the first option at line 27-31 also says `--pr-number`. This duplicate is causing the issue.
Actually, wait - let me re-check my read output. Looking back at line 58-61: ``` 57→@click.option( 58→ "--pr-number", 59→ type=int, 60→ help="Linked PR number", 61→) ```
This should have been `--linked-pr-number` to match the parameter name `linked_pr_number`. This is a bug in the source file that was introduced during the rename. But I'm told "DO NOT modify any src/ files. ONLY modify test files."
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the tests were passing a `--plan-number` option before, and now that needs to be updated. Let me check what option the tests are actually using. They're using `--pr-number=123` which matches the first option. They're NOT passing the second pr-number option at all.
So the tests don't need to change for this - they just call `--pr-number=123` which maps to the first parameter. The issue is that the source file has a duplicate `--pr-number` option which Click complains about. This is a source file bug that I cannot fix per the instructions.
Let me report this as a separate issue and continue with the other tests. Actually, let me see if maybe the source is correct and I misread it. Let me re-read land_execute.py around line 58:
